debt_items.description: SaveLogsToDatabaseAsync at lines 150-160 never persists changes. The method creates a new ClaudeManagerDbContext, removes existing StreamedLines records, but lacks the critical SaveChangesAsync() call. Without this, all database modifications are silently discarded when the using block ends. The data loss risk is high since this is the only persistence mechanism for build logs.

RefreshLogs() early returns at lines 90-95 when logs are empty, but the method should Refresh logs by calling FetchExecutionDetailAsync per PRD. This suggests the method may be relying on external refreshes instead of actively pulling execution details.

Both CopyLogsToClipboard (lines 183-192) and DownloadLogs (lines 220-229) have identical try-catch-finally structures with JS and base exception handlers. Could be refactored into a shared handler to reduce code duplication.

summary: BLOCKING issue identified: SaveLogsToDatabaseAsync method (lines 148-161) removes existing records but never calls SaveChangesAsync(), causing complete data loss. Since this method is only called as part of RefreshLogs(), the log data is never persisted to database despite the method's design intent. The method must include await dbContext.StreamedLines.SaveChangesAsync(); at the end before the using statement closes.
